mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains at very low levels, equity discount rates stay compressed and risk assets reprice upward. This should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month because lower policy rates reduce the hurdle rate on future cash flows and support multiples.
regime_hint: easing or near-zero policy rate regime
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline when FEDFUNDS is flat-to-down, or if the sign turns negative in a rolling 3-year window with at least 20 triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions, raises discount rates, and pressures equity valuations. Under this regime, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative.
evidence_refs: fact:policy-rate tightening transmits to higher discount rates
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean after threshold crossings.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income growth and slower demand, which should weigh on earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the sign of the rolling correlation is positive for more than 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO captures improving industrial supply utilization and stronger corporate revenue expectations. That should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, especially when the move is broad and not just a one-month rebound.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the hit rate falls below 50% in expanding production regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates simultaneous labor income growth and retail demand growth, which should raise earnings revisions and push the S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when both series improve together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS does not outperform each series alone in explaining 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered joint-expansion periods fail to beat baseline by at least 5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A housing upturn reflected by rising HOUST transmits into construction activity, consumer confidence, and related demand channels. If HOUST turns up after weakness, S&P 500 returns over the next month should be positive.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change crosses above 0 after a prior decline
failure_condition: Reject if upward HOUST turn signals are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the average forward return is indistinguishable from zero after the crossing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with firm payroll gains indicates a synchronized real-economy expansion that supports earnings and risk appetite. In that setting, S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next quarter, but only when both growth and hiring improve together.
evidence_refs: fact:real activity and employment co-move in expansions
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between GDPC1 growth and PAYEMS growth is not positive in rolling tests, or if simultaneous improvements do not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real policy rates fall and the market anticipates policy accommodation. That combination should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as discount-rate pressure eases.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-overshoot episodes with subdued FEDFUNDS do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in out-of-sample tests.
